title: [Design Doc] config file parsing changes for podman 6
body:
As part of podman 6 we like to improve and consolidate how we parse our various config files.


#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Paul, this LGTM.  I had just a nit and you answered my other questions already.   My one concern about this document is there are a couple of loose ends (JSON files, and containers.conf split) that I don't want to loose.  Could you add a section at the end where we can cite follow-up items?

This was excellent work BTW.  Thank you.
Can I get another review and merge here? I need to start working on the implementation
I didnt see any major objections.  As such, I'm willing to merge.
